articles.title: Colleen Wenke of Taconic Partners: 5 Questions
articles.narrative: Taconic Partners has successfully leased up The Ellery, a 330-unit luxury apartment tower in Times Square, demonstrating the viability of residential development in previously non-traditional neighborhoods. According to Colleen Wenke, president and COO, the project's success stems from creating a "calm oasis" within the bustling area, featuring amenities like an indoor garden and pool, coupled with excellent mass transit access. This approach caters to a growing demand for living close to work and highlights the potential for converting functionally obsolete Class B office buildings into residential uses to address New York City's housing shortage. Taconic is also seeing strong performance at The Miramar, a mixed-income development in Inwood, which benefits from transit access, waterfront proximity, and relative affordability within Manhattan.

articles.title: The Plan: Suburb Meets City at 487 Henry Street in Brooklyn
articles.narrative: Eckstrom NYC has completed the renovation of 487 Henry Street, a luxurious 6,300-square-foot townhouse in Brooklyn's Cobble Hill neighborhood. The property, which was originally built in the mid-1800s, now features five bedrooms, six bathrooms, and a range of high-end amenities including multiple outdoor spaces, a sauna, cold plunge, and a workout area. The developer aimed to blend city and suburban living, with the property listed for $14.5 million.

data_points.notable_features: Features five bedrooms, six bathrooms, multiple outdoor spaces including a backyard with a 130-year-old American elm tree, a terrace, and a rooftop lounge with an outdoor kitchen and Manhattan views. Amenities include a sauna, cold plunge, and workout space.
data_points.project_notes: Renovations were completed in March 2026 and the property was listed in April 2026. The property spans six levels and has two entrances.

articles.title: Inside the AI Revolution Quietly Reshaping CRE’s Slowest Data Layer
articles.narrative: Crexi has integrated Zoneomics, an AI-enabled zoning data platform, into its analytics product, Crexi Intelligence. This integration allows users to instantly access zoning classifications, permitted uses, and parcel boundary geometry directly on property detail pages, eliminating the need to navigate fragmented municipal databases. Users can now pose plain-language queries about development potential and receive source-backed answers, significantly streamlining the property evaluation process. This move addresses the long-standing challenge of fragmented zoning data in commercial real estate, providing a competitive edge in a rapidly evolving market with increasing legislative changes and office-to-apartment conversions.

articles.title: Trump’s Federal Building Selloff Runs Into $26B Repair Backlog
articles.narrative: The Trump administration's initiative to reduce the federal government's real estate portfolio is facing significant challenges due to a substantial backlog in building maintenance. The U.S. General Services Administration (GSA) has identified nearly $26 billion in repair needs across its properties, with some estimates placing the total federal backlog at over $50 billion. This issue, coupled with lengthy congressional approval processes for major repair projects, is hindering efforts to consolidate office space and divest underutilized assets. GSA Administrator Ed Forst noted that strategic investments in existing buildings are often necessary before properties can be effectively consolidated or sold.

articles.title: Delshah Capital Buys 227 and 456 Grand Street in Williamsburg for $85M
articles.narrative: Delshah Capital has acquired two mixed-use residential properties in Williamsburg, Brooklyn, from Bronstein Properties for a combined $85 million. The transaction included 227 Grand Street and 456 Grand Street, with the latter benefiting from a 421-a tax abatement. The acquisition was financed with a $60.5 million loan from Prospect Ridge.

data_points.notable_features: 456 Grand Street includes a 421-a tax abatement expiring in 2030.
data_points.project_notes: 456 Grand Street was constructed in 2015 under New York City's PlaNYC Brownfield Cleanup Program. Bronstein acquired 227 Grand Street in a 2022 bankruptcy sale.

articles.title: MetroNational Buys 244-Room Moran Hotel at CITYCENTRE in West Houston
articles.narrative: MetroNational, a local owner-operator, has acquired The Moran Hotel, a 244-room property located within the 47-acre CITYCENTRE mixed-use development in West Houston. The hotel recently underwent renovations and features extended-stay accommodations, a pool, fitness center, and extensive meeting and event space. JLL represented the seller, Midway, in this transaction.

data_points.notable_features: Located within the 47-acre CITYCENTRE mixed-use development; recently renovated; offers extended-stay accommodations, a pool, fitness center, and 15,750 square feet of meeting and event space.

articles.title: JLL Negotiates Sale of Retail Strip Center Portfolio in Dallas-Fort Worth, Waco
articles.narrative: JLL has brokered the sale of a five-property retail strip center portfolio located across the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex and Waco. N3 Real Estate sold the assets to Curbline Properties Corp. The transaction highlights continued investor interest in well-located retail properties within Texas markets.

articles.title: Outdoor Wireless Networks Signs 42,362 SF Industrial Lease in Richardson, Texas
articles.narrative: Outdoor Wireless Networks LLC has secured a 42,362-square-foot industrial lease at Parc North East in Richardson, Texas. This transaction provides the tenant with significant industrial space within a modern, four-building development in a key Dallas suburb. The lease was facilitated by Colliers representing the tenant and Holt Lunsford Commercial representing the landlord.

data_points.notable_features: Part of a four-building, 434,640-square-foot industrial development.

articles.title: Continental Battery Systems to Open 25,000 SF Headquarters in Irving
articles.narrative: Continental Battery Systems, a supplier of batteries for various sectors, has signed a 10-year lease for a new 25,793-square-foot headquarters in Irving, TX. This move is expected to create approximately 95 new jobs in the local economy. The company plans to occupy the space in the coming weeks.

data_points.address: 750 N. John Carpenter Freeway, Irving, TX

articles.title: Big tech returning to the office — on its own terms
articles.narrative: A recent Newmark report, "Big Tech's U.S. Office Strategy Is Clear: Concentrate, Upgrade, Expand," indicates that major technology companies are no longer broadly reducing their office footprints. Instead, they are strategically expanding in key innovation markets, focusing on consolidating employees into premier buildings, and upgrading their work environments. This shift emphasizes quality over quantity, with tech firms favoring flagship campuses and trophy assets that offer deep labor pools and strong innovation ecosystems, particularly driven by the need for in-person collaboration in the AI sector. The report suggests that while space utilization per employee may be slightly less than pre-pandemic, the overall reduction is smaller than anticipated, as companies plan for peak attendance days, leading to continued demand for substantial footprints.
articles.market_intelligence: High-quality Class A and trophy buildings continue to outperform lower-tier properties, with tenants gravitating toward buildings offering superior amenities, transit access, and collaborative environments. Older Class B properties continue to face elevated vacancy and leasing challenges. New office construction has slowed significantly, helping bring supply and demand into better balance.

articles.title: Colliers closes sale of 324,800-square-foot industrial facility in Indianapolis
articles.narrative: Colliers has brokered the sale of County Line Commerce Park Building II, a 324,880-square-foot industrial facility in Indianapolis. The property, developed by a joint venture between Citimark and Gershman Partners, was acquired by Alfa Laval, which uses it as its North American manufacturing, testing, and R&D headquarters. This transaction represents the first completed building within the larger County Line Commerce Park, a master-planned industrial development.

data_points.property_name: County Line Commerce Park Building II
data_points.notable_features: Features 36-foot clear heights, 25 dock-high doors, 330 parking spaces, and building systems designed for advanced manufacturing and R&D operations, with immediate access to Interstate 65.
data_points.project_notes: This is the first completed building within the 117-acre, two-phase County Line Commerce Park, which is planned to include four industrial buildings totaling approximately 1.34 million square feet, two retail outlots, and a hotel site.

articles.title: Peerless Development begins pre-leasing at 1880 N. Milwaukee apartment development in Chicago
articles.narrative: Peerless Development has commenced pre-leasing for 1880 Milwaukee, a new 44-unit boutique apartment community in Chicago's Bucktown neighborhood, with first residents expected in August 2026. The five-story mid-rise will offer one- and two-bedroom floor plans, along with 3,200 square feet of ground-level retail space. The development is designed to complement the neighborhood's character and offers strong transit-oriented access, addressing the demand for contemporary housing in an area with limited new multifamily construction.

data_points.notable_features: Transit-oriented development with rooftop deck, reserved garage parking with EV charging, and bicycle storage. Select units offer private balconies.
data_points.project_notes: Rents start at $2,325 per month for one-bedroom apartments and $3,645 per month for two-bedroom residences.

articles.title: Streetlights Residential developing 22-story multifamily development in Plano
articles.narrative: StreetLights Residential is developing a 22-story luxury multifamily high-rise and three adjacent townhomes in Plano, TX. The project, located at 6501 Legacy Drive, will feature 261 high-rise residences and three townhomes for lease, with construction slated to begin in June 2026 and completion expected in February 2029. The development aims to offer a refined, hospitality-driven living experience with extensive amenities, appealing to discerning renters seeking larger floorplans and a maintenance-free lifestyle in a connected outdoor environment.

data_points.notable_features: 22-story luxury high-rise with three adjacent townhomes, located within the 100-acre former JCPenney headquarters campus, offering walkable access to Legacy West and immediate access to walking trails, city parks, dog parks, and pickleball courts.
data_points.project_notes: High-rise residences will range from 682 to 2,843 square feet. Townhomes will be three-bedroom, three-and-a-half-bath layouts ranging from 3,283 to 3,316 square feet. Construction is scheduled to begin in June 2026.

articles.title: Apollo lending REIT to dissolve after $9B offload
articles.narrative: Apollo Commercial Real Estate Finance, a lending REIT and subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, is dissolving and liquidating its holdings after offloading nearly all of its loan commitments. The move follows the sale of a $9 billion loan portfolio to Athene Holding, another Apollo subsidiary, earlier this year. The sale, which closed in April, left the REIT with approximately $2.2 billion in assets, primarily cash, and only two remaining loans totaling $146 million. The company's CEO stated that the dissolution is in the best interest of the company, highlighting a trend where insurance companies are becoming significant capital providers in the real estate industry.

articles.title: Michael Levitt, affordable housing pioneer, dies at 94
articles.narrative: Michael Levitt, a pioneer in affordable housing development and founder of The Michaels Organization, recently passed away at the age of 94. Levitt was instrumental in embracing government-assisted housing programs after the 1974 Housing and Community Development Act, utilizing tax credits and public housing revitalization initiatives. His company grew to manage a portfolio of over 75,000 units and $12 billion in assets, spanning various housing types across the nation. Levitt was also recognized for establishing the first scholarship program and on-site supportive services for affordable housing residents.

articles.title: Cress Capital offloads Denver-area office building at 32% discount
articles.narrative: Dunton Commercial and Laminar Real Estate have acquired Union Tower, a 205,800-square-foot office building in Lakewood, CO, for nearly $17.3 million. The seller, Cress Capital, offloaded the property at a 32% discount from its 2017 sale price. The buyers plan to renovate the 73% occupied, 10-story building with new amenities and spec suites, aiming to capitalize on the market's flight-to-quality trend.

data_points.notable_features: Tallest office building in Lakewood.
data_points.project_notes: Sold at a 32% discount from its 2017 sale price of $25.3 million.

articles.market_intelligence: Denver's office market vacancy was over 25% across the metro in Q1, with downtown rates at 34%.

articles.title: NYC’s top construction permits: Week ending June 12, 2026
articles.narrative: The Gotham Organization has filed plans for a significant mixed-use development in Brooklyn's East New York, comprising three buildings with 599 residential units and over 533,000 square feet of space. Other notable new building permits include a 23-story mixed-use tower in Long Island City by ZD Jasper and a 99-unit project in Greenpoint by Mendy Schwimmer. Major alteration permits were filed for St. Catherine Academy in the Bronx and two East Village properties by L&M Development. Demolition permits were also issued for a large property in Jamaica, Queens, and multiple structures in the Bronx and East Harlem.

articles.title: The Daily Dirt: FARE Act returns just $7 per complaint to consumers
articles.narrative: This article critiques the effectiveness of the FARE Act in its first year, highlighting a significant disparity between the number of complaints filed and the actual financial recoveries for renters. Despite over 2,000 complaints about illegal broker fees, only about $7 per complaint was returned to consumers, totaling $15,500. The author argues that this minimal recovery, representing only 1 in 100 complaints, indicates the act is not effectively addressing the issue, possibly because many complaints did not involve actual payments or were directed at brokers rather than landlords. The piece also touches on the Community Opportunity to Purchase Act and the challenges of developing a parking lot in the East Village due to restrictive R8B zoning, which severely limits potential housing units and revenue for struggling co-ops and NYCHA.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Ghislaine Maxwell’s former Lenox Hill townhouse sells for $17M
articles.narrative: A vacant 0.9-acre lot at 191 Canal Street on Staten Island was the priciest commercial transaction, trading for $10.5 million. Additionally, a townhouse at 115 East 69th Street in Lenox Hill, spanning approximately 10,000 square feet, was acquired by a developer. The property, previously configured as a family office, will be delivered vacant.

data_points.address: 191 Canal Street, New York City, NY

articles.title: AG James sues landlords over rent-stabilized building violations
articles.narrative: New York Attorney General Letitia James has filed lawsuits against two Brooklyn landlords, John Anderson and Claudette Henry, alleging violations of rent stabilization laws. The lawsuits claim the landlords failed to properly register regulated units, attempted illegal evictions, and harassed tenants. These actions are part of a broader compliance program targeting buildings with five or fewer units built before 1974 that were later altered to have six or more units, making them de facto rent-stabilized.

articles.title: HPD unveils fund for supportive housing preservation
articles.narrative: The New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD) has launched a $1 billion Supportive Preservation Program aimed at preserving the city's existing supportive housing stock. This initiative will provide below-market loans, modify existing HPD loans, and offer residential real estate tax exemptions to encourage conventional lenders and address the financial needs of these properties. The program seeks to upgrade aging infrastructure and improve energy efficiency in buildings that have provided affordable housing and services for decades, ensuring their long-term stability and continued support for vulnerable tenants.

data_points.notable_features: Targets existing supportive housing stock for preservation and upgrades, including facade, windows, roof, boiler, and heating system improvements.
data_points.project_notes: The program aims to preserve 200,000 affordable homes over the next decade as part of Mayor Mamdani's housing plan.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: New York City Department of Housing Preservation and Development (HPD)

articles.title: Broward County drops pursuit of Spirit Airlines Dania Beach HQ
articles.narrative: Broward County has withdrawn its motion to bid on a portion of Spirit Airlines' former 615,600-square-foot headquarters in Dania Beach, Florida, which is slated for auction next month. The county had considered acquiring the property for a new government building, citing potential savings of $350 million compared to new construction. However, the motion was withdrawn due to zoning concerns and the lack of prior written approval from Kimco Realty, the owner of the larger mixed-use development Dania Pointe where the headquarters is located. The property is being sold as part of Spirit Airlines' liquidation efforts following its cessation of operations.

data_points.notable_features: Includes office space, parking, training facilities, and temporary housing; part of the larger mixed-use development Dania Pointe.
data_points.project_notes: Broward County officials were willing to pay up to $100 million for the property, excluding the housing component. The property is zoned for office use, requiring approval from Kimco Realty for government use. Non-binding indications of interest are due June 24, with a stalking horse bidder expected by July 8, and a final bid deadline of July 20.

articles.title: “Nonsensical”: Miami-Dade votes to pursue Fisher Island site via eminent domain
articles.narrative: Miami-Dade County Commission has voted to proceed with acquiring the Fisher Island fuel terminal property through eminent domain, despite a previous $400 million negotiation with the current owner, a partnership including HRP Group, The Related Group, and Russell Galbut, having failed. The county aims to secure the nearly 10-acre site, which is critical infrastructure for PortMiami, after having missed an earlier opportunity to purchase it for $180 million. The current owners had planned to demolish the fuel bunk, remediate the land, and develop luxury condominiums, including conveying 4 acres to the Fisher Island Community Association. The eminent domain process is expected to be contentious and potentially costly, with legal experts suggesting the county may pay significantly more than the previously negotiated price.

data_points.notable_features: Fuel depot serving Port Miami, located in one of the priciest ZIP codes in the U.S.
data_points.project_notes: Site was previously acquired for $180 million in 2025 by the current owners. The county had previously approved the eminent domain process in October 2025.

articles.title: “Behind our backs”: Ex-Lt Gov, Florida Rep Carlos Lopez-Cantera, father sued over Miami-Dade land deal
articles.narrative: A lawsuit has been filed against former Florida politician Carlos M. Lopez-Cantera and his father, Carlos C. Lopez-Cantera, alleging they conspired to defraud investment partners in a Miami-Dade County land deal. The suit claims the Lopez-Canteras misled partners about the value of an 8.3-acre vacant tract, inducing them to sell it for $40,000 to Quarry Real Estate Holdings, an entity led by the younger Lopez-Cantera. The plaintiffs allege the Lopez-Canteras concealed negotiations to lift a conservation easement that would significantly increase the property's value, intending to flip the land for millions and deprive investors of higher profits.

data_points.project_notes: The 8.3-acre site was part of a larger 140-acre tract acquired by Pan American West in the early 2000s. A conservation easement was placed on the 8.3-acre portion in 2008 to obtain development approvals for the larger tract. The alleged fraudulent sale price was $40,000, while an appraisal presented to partners valued the land at $33,000.

articles.title: Developer Menachem Kranz sells waterfront Bay Harbor Islands home for $22M
articles.narrative: Developer Menachem Kranz, through 9525 BH 2 LLC, sold a newly constructed 8,000-square-foot waterfront mansion in Bay Harbor Islands, FL for $21.8 million. The sale achieved a record price per square foot for the town at over $2,700. The property, designed by SDH Studio with interiors by Alizée Brion and landscaping by CLAD, was on the market for only six days, reflecting strong demand and limited inventory for high-end waterfront homes in the area.

data_points.address: 9640 West Broadview Drive, Bay Harbor Islands, FL
data_points.notable_features: Waterfront property with 80 feet of water frontage and a dock, includes a 2,250-square-foot rooftop terrace and an air-conditioned two-car garage.
data_points.project_notes: The 0.3-acre site was previously acquired by Menachem Kranz for $6 million in 2022.

articles.market_intelligence: The Bay Harbor Islands market is experiencing high demand and low inventory for turnkey waterfront homes priced above $15 million. The town has seen significant price growth and a wave of new developments post-pandemic.

articles.title: Menashe Properties closes on another Loop tower for $57M
articles.narrative: Menashe Properties has acquired 180 North LaSalle Street, a 38-story office tower in Chicago, for $56.5 million. This marks the firm's third office acquisition in Chicago in four years, demonstrating a strong belief in the market's recovery. The buyer is banking on the "Google effect" from the nearby Thompson Center redevelopment to attract new tenants, with Stream Realty Partners continuing to manage the property's business operations.

data_points.notable_features: Located across from the Thompson Center, which is being redeveloped into Google's Chicago headquarters.
data_points.project_notes: Seller previously acquired the property in 2016 for $198.5 million.

articles.title: Landmark designation of Bryn Mawr Avenue building stalling demolition, development plans
articles.narrative: A proposed 95-unit mixed-use apartment complex in Chicago's Edgewater neighborhood faces a significant hurdle as the Commission on Chicago Landmarks has issued a preliminary landmark recommendation for the Bryn Mawr Avenue area. Developer Harlem Irving Companies, which had planned to demolish three existing buildings for the project, has had one demolition permit denied due to the building's historic significance. The developer has already invested $6 million in the project since acquiring the site in 2020, and the final decision on the demolition permit is expected in July.

data_points.address: 1114 West Bryn Mawr Avenue, Chicago, IL
data_points.project_notes: The developer previously acquired the site in 2020. The project involves the demolition of 1114 West Bryn Mawr Avenue, 1106 West Bryn Mawr Avenue, and 5614 North Winthrop Avenue. The demolition permit for 1114 West Bryn Mawr Avenue was denied due to a preliminary landmark recommendation for the Bryn Mawr Avenue area.

companies_people.label: OWNER
companies_people.firm_name: City of Chicago Department of Planning and Development

articles.title: Related nears marquee tenant for office skyscraper at Fulton Market’s gateway
articles.narrative: Law firm Sidley Austin is reportedly nearing a deal to lease over half of a planned 45-story office skyscraper at 725 West Randolph Street in Chicago's Fulton Market area. The building, developed by Related Midwest, is slated for completion in 2030 and will span 968,000 square feet. This potential lease would make the law firm the anchor tenant, signaling confidence in new, high-quality office space despite broader market trends.

data_points.notable_features: 45-story office skyscraper located near Fulton Market.
data_points.project_notes: Developer pivoted from original mixed-use apartment and hotel plans in 2023 to an office tower. The updated proposal for the office tower has not yet been approved by the Chicago City Council.

articles.market_intelligence: Office space availability in Chicago was at an all-time high in 2023, though the Fulton Market area continued to attract office and retail tenants.

articles.title: SoftBank tied to detail-sparse land purchase in Milam County
articles.narrative: Entities affiliated with SoftBank Group have acquired 4,709 acres in Milam County, Texas, within the larger 33,000-acre former Sandow Lakes Ranch site. While specific plans for the land are not detailed, the purchase is speculated to be related to data center development, given SoftBank's recent investments in the sector and its partnership with OpenAI for a large data center project in the same county. The acquisition comes amidst a surge in data center development in Texas, which has prompted calls for increased regulation from state officials.

data_points.notable_features: Part of the 33,000-acre former Sandow Lakes Ranch site, which includes Alcoa Corporation’s decommissioned aluminum smelter.

articles.market_intelligence: The Austin Business Journal reported that Xebec Holdings' plans for The Switch campus at the Sandow Lakes Ranch site include a 3,300-acre campus slated for tenant readiness in 2026. SoftBank acquired data center investment firm DigitalBridge for $4 billion in late 2025. Governor Greg Abbott has recommended regulations for future data center developments, including requiring them to fund their own grid interconnection and eliminating sales tax exemptions.

articles.title: Terry Black’s BBQ owners plan $63M hotel in San Antonio
articles.narrative: Black Family Hospitality, led by the owners of Terry Black's BBQ, is planning a $63 million hotel and restaurant development in San Antonio. The project, named The Mandeville, will feature a three-story hotel with over 40 rooms, a Terry Black's BBQ outpost, a separate non-barbecue restaurant, and a 9,800-square-foot bathhouse called The Depths. Construction is slated to begin in September and conclude by June 2028.

data_points.notable_features: Located near the Pearl District, the development will include a hotel, two restaurants, a parking garage, and a bathhouse with multiple spas and plunges.
data_points.project_notes: The 1.4-acre site was previously acquired in 2021 by 4M Broadway LLC. The bathhouse, The Depths, is a separate component with an estimated construction cost of $9 million.

articles.title: DataBank lands more than $1B boost for Red Oak data center megacampus
articles.narrative: DataBank has secured an additional $1.45 billion in financing for its Red Oak data center megacampus, bringing the total project funding to $2.65 billion. This new capital includes an $800 million revolving credit facility and a $650 million upsizing for the Red Oak campus. The development, which previously received a $2 billion construction loan for its initial phase, is part of a larger trend of significant data center expansion in the Dallas area.

data_points.phase_1: first three of eight planned data centers
data_points.notable_features: The campus is planned for eight data centers with 180 megawatts of power capacity.
data_points.project_notes: The first three buildings are already fully leased. This financing follows a $2 billion construction loan received in April for the first phase.

articles.market_intelligence: Red Oak is emerging as a data center hub, with Compass Datacenters adding over 750,000 square feet across three buildings by 2026, and Google establishing a $600 million data center in 2023. Texas is projected to become the largest data center market in the U.S. by 2030, with grid demand from data centers expected to reach 40 gigawatts by 2028, up from 8 gigawatts in 2025. As of January, Texas had 387 operational data centers.

articles.title: Monty Bennett’s Ashford leads bid for bankrupt River Walk hotel amid Braemar divorce
articles.narrative: Ashford Hospitality is poised to acquire the bankrupt 145 Navarro Street property in San Antonio for $32 million, with GrayStreet Partners serving as a backup buyer at $30 million. The former office building was undergoing a conversion into a luxury Marriott Autograph Collection hotel, but the project faced significant delays and cost overruns due to extensive flood and mold damage, leading the owner, Blueprint Hospitality, to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. A new owner is expected to be finalized by mid-July.

data_points.project_notes: The property was previously acquired from CPS Energy and was undergoing conversion into a Marriott Autograph Collection hotel. The project faced significant water damage and mold issues, leading to bankruptcy. GrayStreet affiliate ERC Acquisitions IV LLC was a lender for the project.

articles.title: SF office leasing activity rides AI wave to near 30-year high
articles.narrative: San Francisco's office market is experiencing a significant resurgence, with leasing activity nearing a 30-year high, primarily fueled by the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence firms. The first half of the year saw approximately 6.4 million square feet leased, with a projected annual volume of 15.3 million square feet, a pace comparable to 1996. This surge is directly linked to a substantial increase in venture capital funding for AI startups, which has more than doubled compared to last year, driving strong tenant demand. The demand has also shifted tenant preferences towards fully built-out, immediately occupiable spaces, leading to a significant reduction in available sublease inventory.
articles.market_intelligence: Venture capital funding has historically been a strong driver of tenant demand in San Francisco's tech sector. The current AI boom is also impacting the local housing market, leading to rising rents and home prices, tight supply, and rapid trading of high-end residences.

articles.title: $25M Pac Heights Victorian trades after less than two weeks on market
articles.narrative: A historic Queen Anne Victorian home in San Francisco's Pacific Heights neighborhood sold for approximately $25 million in an all-cash transaction. The property, which had been owned by the same family for nearly 60 years, was on the market for less than two weeks before going under contract. The sale highlights the tight luxury housing market in San Francisco, driven by increasing wealth from the tech sector and a limited supply of high-end, move-in ready homes.

data_points.notable_features: Nine-bed, eight-bath Queen Anne Victorian home, 149 years old, well-preserved, chosen as the exhibition house for San Francisco's 2026 Decorator Showcase.
data_points.project_notes: The buyer agreed to pay both sides of the broker commission and the real estate transfer tax, bringing the total cost to approximately $25 million. The property was last acquired in 1969 for $100,000.

articles.market_intelligence: San Francisco's inventory of homes for sale fell to 900 in May, down by 500 homes year-over-year, marking the first time since 2019 that available homes in May dropped below 1,000. Sales from January to May are up 8 percent year-over-year in the city, fueled by wealth created by the artificial intelligence industry. The median home price in the city neared $2.2 million earlier this year, an all-time high.

articles.title: Adult website founder sells three SF buildings amid legal battle
articles.narrative: The founder of an adult entertainment website sold three commercial buildings in San Francisco for a combined $6.7 million to an affiliate of Urban Rock Properties. The sale occurred amidst an ongoing legal dispute involving the seller over an easement violation at a separate property, with the plaintiff's attorney suggesting the sales were an attempt to divest assets securing a judgment.

data_points.project_notes: The property at 201 Ninth Street formerly hosted the AsiaSF nightclub and is slated to become a new nightclub called 201 Bodega.

articles.title: SFA Agency Extends, Expands Tenancy at One Battery Park Plaza
articles.narrative: SFA Agency LLC, an affiliate of Northwestern Mutual, has extended its lease and expanded its footprint at One Battery Park Plaza in Lower Manhattan. The financial services company will now occupy 14,071 square feet in a 10-year lease extension, adding 5,170 square feet to its existing space. This transaction highlights the continued demand for high-quality, well-located office space in Lower Manhattan, bringing the building to near full occupancy.

data_points.project_notes: SFA Agency has been a tenant since 2023 and will relocate within the building in early 2027.

articles.title: DTLA’s Bank of America Plaza Trades for $210M in Receivership Sale
articles.narrative: The Bank of America Plaza, a 1.4-million-square-foot office tower in Downtown Los Angeles, has been sold for $210 million in a receivership sale. Capital Group, a long-term anchor tenant in the building, acquired the property. This transaction marks the largest office sale in Los Angeles in 2026 and the largest in Los Angeles County since 2023. Capital Group plans to expand its occupancy within the building, consolidating its local workforce and making significant investments to enhance the workplace experience.

data_points.address: 333 S. Hope St., Los Angeles, CA
data_points.project_notes: Fee-simple title was delivered at closing through a receivership sale.

articles.title: LaSalle Acquires Student Housing Complex Near Michigan State University
articles.narrative: LaSalle Investment Management, on behalf of its LaSalle Value Partners IX fund, has acquired Red Cedar Flats, a 190-unit, 436-bed student housing community in East Lansing, Michigan. The property, completed in 2021, is strategically located near Michigan State University. This acquisition marks the fund's initial foray into the student housing sector, with LaSalle noting the attractive fundamentals of campus-adjacent assets at growing universities with limited new development.

data_points.notable_features: Purpose-built student housing community located within walking distance to Michigan State University.
data_points.project_notes: Acquired below replacement cost with in-place cash flow.

articles.title: Bradford Allen Closes 60K-SF Office Lease in Lincolnshire
articles.narrative: Bradford Allen has completed a 60,000-square-foot office lease renewal with an office supply retailer at 300 Tri State International Drive in Lincolnshire, Illinois. The property is part of the Tri State Lincolnshire office complex, which Bradford Allen has owned and operated since 2017, investing over $30 million in significant renovations and upgrades to enhance the tenant experience and building infrastructure.

data_points.address: 300 Tri State International Drive, Lincolnshire, IL
data_points.notable_features: Part of a five-building, 560,000-square-foot office complex with a 135-seat auditorium and on-site fitness center, offering convenient access to downtown Chicago and O'Hare International Airport.
data_points.project_notes: Bradford Allen has invested over $30 million in improvements since acquiring the complex in 2017, including upgrades to lobbies, conference rooms, elevators, roofs, and cooling towers.

articles.title: Oil Crisis Highlights Plastics' Hidden Costs For Construction
articles.narrative: The ongoing Middle East conflict has highlighted the construction industry's increasing reliance on petroleum-based plastics, which account for nearly 20% of global plastic production and are vital for various building components. While a weak residential market has somewhat mitigated the impact on finished plastic product prices, raw material costs for plastics like PVC, polypropylene, and polyethylene have surged due to rising oil prices. Experts note that while plastics are not yet the primary driver of overall construction costs compared to materials like steel or copper, their growing use, particularly in specialized applications like PVC membrane roofs for multifamily and data center projects, makes their cost a significant and increasing factor. Continued oil market uncertainty could lead to further price increases and potential supply shortages, underscoring the industry's vulnerability to petrochemical market fluctuations.

articles.title: Senate Advances Housing Bill After Striking Deal With House Leaders
articles.narrative: The Senate has advanced the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, a significant federal housing bill that has garnered bipartisan support and is expected to pass both chambers. The legislation, which aims to increase housing supply and improve affordability, represents a compromise after extensive negotiations, notably excluding controversial build-to-rent (BTR) regulations that would have mandated sales to single-family buyers within seven years. While the bill still includes provisions to curb large investors' purchases of single-family homes, some ambiguity remains regarding the resale of BTR properties. Proponents believe the bill, despite being a collection of smaller measures, will be a crucial step towards addressing housing challenges across the U.S.

articles.title: Sidley Austin Set To Anchor New 1M SF Related Midwest Office Tower
articles.narrative: Law firm Sidley Austin has signed on as the anchor tenant for Related Midwest's planned 45-story trophy office tower at 725 W. Randolph St. in Chicago's Fulton Market. The firm will occupy more than half of the 1 million-square-foot building, with an expected move-in date in late 2030. This significant lease marks a major step forward for new office development in Chicago, signaling renewed confidence in the market after a period of limited construction activity.

data_points.notable_features: 45-story trophy office building designed by Kohn Pedersen Fox Associates.
data_points.project_notes: The project requires city council approval. Related Midwest previously proposed a mixed-use residential-and-hotel tower on the site in 2023. Construction is expected to begin in 2027.

articles.market_intelligence: The vacancy rate in Chicago's 20 newest office buildings is around 8%. Trophy office peak day occupancy hit 93.7% on March 3, according to Kastle data reported by Newmark.

articles.title: Data Center Moratorium On Gov. Hochul's Desk Puts Dozens Of Projects In Crosshairs
articles.narrative: New York's legislature has passed a bill proposing a one-year moratorium on new data center permits for facilities over 20 MW, which now awaits Governor Kathy Hochul's signature. This legislation, known as the Responsible Data Center Development Act, would also impose new energy efficiency goals, labor standards, and community benefit requirements for projects over 5 MW, and mandate public hearings for all new developments. The move comes despite Governor Hochul's prior initiatives to attract AI and advanced technology infrastructure, including $10 billion in tax credits for semiconductor manufacturing and a $10 billion nanotechnology research facility, which have spurred significant data center development interest. Industry experts note that the 20 MW threshold is low and would impact nearly all new data center projects, while proponents argue the pause is necessary to assess environmental impacts like energy and water usage, and to give communities a voice in development.

articles.market_intelligence: New York has the second-highest commercial electricity rate in North America at 14 cents per kilowatt-hour. As of the second half of 2025, at least 70% of New York’s electricity is estimated to come from renewables.

articles.title: 'We've Taken Control Of Our Destiny': QuadReal Unveils Plan For BTR Growth
articles.narrative: QuadReal Property Group, the real estate arm of British Columbia Investment Management Corporation, is significantly expanding its build-to-rent (BTR) and purpose-built student accommodation (PBSA) portfolios in the UK and Ireland. This strategic growth follows its acquisition of long-term partner Realstar and its resident-facing brand Uncle last year, creating a vertically integrated residential business in Europe. The company plans to grow through both acquisitions of operational real estate and selective development, leveraging its global experience and focusing on direct operational control to manage risk and enhance service offerings. QuadReal recently announced a £120M forward-funding deal for a 241-unit BTR development in Woolwich, London, and is actively exploring further opportunities in markets like Dublin, Leeds, Manchester, and Birmingham, driven by strong fundamentals and acute undersupply in the living sectors.

data_points.notable_features: Part of a larger development creating 700 homes, including 337 affordable properties.
data_points.project_notes: QuadReal will forward-fund the development, which will be managed by its Uncle brand. Construction is expected to begin in spring 2026.
